A) the ones that were transferred to PS I
from PS II
B) electrons from the water splatted.
C) electrons excited when PS I absorbs
light.
D) All of these
45. Electrons from PS-I move downhill to a
molecule of energy-rich- (Pg. 212, E)
A) NADP! B) NAD!
C) FAD* D) GTP
46. The Z scheme is named so because-
(Pg. 212, E)
A) it was discovered by a scientist with Z’
as initial letter of name
B) the carriers of ETS present in thylakoid
membrane are in Z’ shape.
C) it forms Z’ shape when the carriers of
ETS are arranged in sequence on redox
potential scale.
D) both A & C

Identify the correct site for (Pg. 212, E)
I- water splitting, II-NADP* reduction, III-
ATP synthesis
I II III
A) I I III
B) II I III
C) II II I
D) II I II
13.6.1 Splitting of Water
48. Splitting of water is important-
(Pg. 212, E)
A) for the O; formation
B) for the electrons released
C) for the hydroxide ions released
D) for the Hz released
49. Water splitting is associated with-
